item.title: Would Congress’ New Housing Bill Lower Home Prices?
item.description: President Donald Trump’s decision to back out of signing a major housing bill provided a dramatic last-minute twist to lawmakers’ efforts to address Americans’ cost-of-living concerns. The 21st Century Road to Housing Act, which passed both chambers of Congress by a huge margin, is designed to spur housing construction and, in the long run, bring relief to consumers struggling with lofty prices and mortgage rates.

item.title: Mexico Holds Key Rate, Pledges to Keep It at 6.5% for Longer
item.description: Mexico’s central bank held its benchmark interest rate unchanged and signaled it will keep it at the current level for longer as it gages the impact of recent price shocks on inflation.

item.title: Bank of Canada Urged to Use Fewer Core Gauges, Focus on Headline Inflation
item.description: The Bank of Canada is being urged to focus on headline inflation instead of abstracting to core measures when discussing their policy decisions as it sought feedback on its mandate renewal.

item.title: Endless Shrimp Deal Was Scheme to Squeeze Red Lobster, Suit Says
item.description: Red Lobster’s $20 all-you-can-eat shrimp deal wasn’t just a badly designed promotion that inadvertently helped tip the iconic American restaurant chain into bankruptcy.

item.title: More People Worked From Home in 2025 Despite RTO Mandates
item.description: More US employees worked from home in 2025 than the year prior, a small but notable shift that suggests companies’ years-long efforts to get workers back into the office have hit a wall.

item.title: Fed’s Goolsbee Sees Glimmers of Hope In Latest Inflation Report
item.description: Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago President Austan Goolsbee said he saw hopeful signs in the latest inflation report, but warned that price pressures overall remain too high.

item.title: EU Extends Tariff Suspension in Boeing-Airbus Trade Dispute
item.description: European Union member states agreed to extend the suspension of tariffs set to hit $4 billion of American products related to a trade dispute between Airbus SE and Boeing Co., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: NYC ‘Classical Christian’ School Wins UWS Campus for $33 Million
item.description: Geneva School of Manhattan — an institution that describes itself as a “classical Christian school” — is acquiring the former Upper West Side home of a bankrupt private school for $33.2 million.

item.title: Waymo Registers German Entity for Future European Expansion
item.description: Waymo is planning to offer its driverless robotaxis in Germany, the latest move by the Alphabet Inc. unit to expand outside the US.

item.title: Fire Breaks Out at Delta Air Lines Refinery in Pennsylvania
item.description: A fire broke out at Delta Air Lines Inc.’s refinery in Trainer, Pennsylvania, on Thursday.

item.title: Stellantis, Nissan in Talks to Buy Assets From Troubled Marelli
item.description: Stellantis NV and Nissan Motor Co. are in talks to take over some of the assets of Marelli Holdings Co., the global auto-parts maker that’s mired in a challenging reorganization in bankruptcy.

item.title: Hertz Heads for Record Weekly Drop on Funding Plan, Profit Guide
item.description: A record-setting rout in Hertz Global Holdings Inc. stock is continuing for a second day.

item.title: Microsoft Raises Xbox Console Prices for Third Time in 13 Months
item.description: Microsoft Corp. announced a third substantial price increase for the company’s current-generation Xbox video-game consoles in a glaring example of the component shortage crisis that has universally driven up the cost of consumer tech products.

item.title: Michael Saylor’s Misfiring Funding Model Jolts Bitcoin Traders
item.description: Fears that Michael Saylor’s Bitcoin buying machine is beginning to seize up are spilling across the crypto market, fueling the latest leg of Bitcoin’s selloff and exposing cracks in one of the financial structures that has underpinned demand for the world’s largest cryptocurrency over the past two years.

item.title: Macflation Is Officially Here
item.description: Gadget prices inflate.

item.title: Apple to Skip High-End M6 Mac Chips in Favor of AI-Focused M7 Line
item.description: Apple Inc. is making one of the biggest-ever changes to its Mac silicon strategy, preparing to jump ahead to a new artificial intelligence-focused chip generation for its next top-end processors.

item.title: SpaceX Credit Derivatives Start to Trade After Debut Bond Sale
item.description: Credit-default swaps tied to Elon Musk’s SpaceX have begun actively trading after the company sold high-grade bonds this week for the first time, according to people familiar with the matter, allowing investors to hedge against potential losses or to speculate on the creditworthiness of the firm.

item.title: Airfares Are Staying High Even as the Jet Fuel Crisis Fades
item.description: A historic jet fuel crisis is now fading as prices fall globally, but US passengers are yet to see the benefit with airfares still elevated.

item.title: Trump Requests $11.1 Billion in New Aid for Struggling Farmers
item.description: President Donald Trump had a surprise for the farmers he invited for dinner at the White House later Thursday: a request to Congress for $11.1 billion in assistance that would amount to the second bailout of the year for the beleaguered agriculture sector.

item.title: Trump Sons Invested in Private Fund Making Millions on Microcaps
item.description: A fund that includes President Donald Trump’s eldest sons among its investors has booked hundreds of millions of dollars in paper profits through a lucrative playbook that involves taking stakes in a steady stream of small, thinly traded public companies.

item.title: India Says US Trade Deal Hinges on Competitive Edge Over Peers
item.description: India is open to a trade deal with the US provided it offers the nation an edge over peers, commerce minister Piyush Goyal said, signaling that key hurdles remain to an interim agreement.

item.title: Sonos Cuts 3% of Staff Across User Experience, Product Teams
item.description: Sonos Inc. made cuts across its user experience, product and design teams, citing a need to streamline the organization and operate more efficiently.

item.title: US Airline Stocks Erase Pandemic-Era Losses as Oil Prices Ease
item.description: A popular gauge of the US airline industry has finally recovered from pandemic-era losses after six years, as progress on a peace deal between US and Iran pushed oil prices lower and eased the pressure on carriers’ profitability.

item.title: Data Center Surge Brings Risk for States and Munis, Moody’s Says
item.description: A flood of data center projects with their heavy power and water demands are introducing credit risks for state and local governments, according to Moody’s Ratings.

item.title: Three New ETFs to Watch
item.description: The SpaceX IPO set off a predictable frenzy—we look at who may lead the pack. Also, betting against AI, high-yield bonds, plus bonus mentions.

item.title: Cerebras On Track for Record Two-Day Loss as Outlook Disappoints
item.description: Cerebras Systems Inc. shares tumbled Thursday — and were on track for a record two-session loss — after the chipmaker gave a disappointing annual sales outlook.

item.title: Billionaire Adani to Invest $2.1 Billion to Build Airport Cities
item.description: Billionaire Gautam Adani’s airport unit will invest 200 billion rupees ($2.1 billion) to build integrated cities across its network as the ports-to-power conglomerate expands into non-aviation businesses.

item.title: Rio Tinto in Talks With Vitol Over Venture to Cut Freight Costs
item.description: Rio Tinto Group is in talks with oil-trading giant Vitol Group about setting up a freight and logistics joint venture, less than five months after ending talks to buy rival miner and trader Glencore Plc.

item.title: Naval Group Reports Ship Struck in Hormuz as Vessels Turn Back
item.description: A UK naval group said it received a report that a vessel was hit by an unknown projectile in the Strait of Hormuz, hours after several freighters turned around while attempting to cross the vital waterway.

item.title: DeepSeek Plans to Double Staff Across All Departments
item.description: China’s DeepSeek, fresh off a blockbuster fundraising, said it is working to at least double the size of all departments, as it steps up efforts to compete with domestic rivals and global leaders such as OpenAI and Anthropic.
